panels.description: Gateway-level errors broken down by destination model (LLMInferenceService) and HTTP response code. Maps ingress failures to specific models to identify routing or auth issues per service.
panels.title: Gateway Errors by Model Server & Response Code

panels.description: Envoy response flags indicating transport-level issues. DC=downstream connection terminated, NR=no route found, DPE=downstream protocol error, UH=no healthy upstream, UO=upstream overflow.
panels.title: Gateway Response Flags

panels.description: Kuadrant authorization gateway decisions: allowed, denied, and error rates. These are cluster-wide counters (no per-model labels available from Kuadrant).
panels.title: Kuadrant Auth Decisions

panels.description: P95 gateway latency broken down by HTTP response code. Fast errors (e.g., 401 auth rejection) vs slow errors (e.g., 503 timeout) help distinguish failure modes.
panels.title: Gateway Latency by Response Code (P95)

panels.description: P95 gateway latency broken down by Envoy response flags. Flags indicate failure modes: DC (downstream connection), NR (no route), DPE (downstream protocol error), UH (upstream unhealthy), UO (upstream overflow).
panels.title: Gateway Latency by Response Flags (P95)

panels.description: HTTP error rate over time. Shows non-2xx responses indicating service-level failures.
panels.title: HTTP Request Rate (Success vs Error)

panels.description: vLLM request outcomes by finished_reason. 'abort' = engine-level failures, 'stop' = normal completion, 'length' = hit max_tokens limit.
panels.title: vLLM Request Outcomes

panels.description: Controller reconciliation errors in the scheduling layer. Broken down by controller: inferencemodel (routing rules), inferencepool (pool management), pod (pod lifecycle).
panels.title: Scheduling Failures (Controller Errors)

panels.description: Scheduler workqueue depth and retry rate. High depth indicates the scheduler is falling behind. High retries indicate repeated failures.
panels.title: Scheduling Workqueue Health

panels.description: Preemption rate and max KV cache utilization. Preemptions happen when the engine evicts in-flight requests due to KV cache exhaustion. Sustained preemptions = insufficient serving capacity.
panels.title: Memory Pressure (Preemptions + KV Cache)

panels.description: Request abort rate split by phase (prefill vs decode). Identifies whether failures concentrate in a specific phase of disaggregated deployments.
panels.title: Abort Rate by Phase (Prefill vs Decode)

panels.description: Request abort rate by component type (workload, workload-leader, workload-worker, etc.). In Wide EP deployments, shows whether failures concentrate on leaders or workers.
panels.title: Abort Rate by Component (Leader/Worker)

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate over time. Dropping hit rate indicates workload pattern change or cache eviction pressure.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Scheduler-side prefix indexer size. If not growing with traffic, prefix-aware routing may not be functioning.
panels.title: Prefix Indexer Size

panels.description: Compares queue time (scheduling delay) vs inference time (compute time) split by phase. queue_time >> inference_time = insufficient replicas / scheduling delay. inference_time high + queue_time low = engine bottleneck.
panels.title: Queue Time vs Inference Time by Phase (P95)

panels.description: Scheduler pool-level metrics: average KV cache utilization, average queue size, and ready pod count. Provides a scheduler-eye view of pool health.
panels.title: Scheduler Pool Utilization

panels.description: Scheduler-level errors broken down by error code. Shows which specific error types are occurring at the scheduling layer.
panels.title: Scheduler Errors by Error Code

panels.description: Scheduling attempt outcomes by status (success/failure). High failure rate indicates scheduler cannot find suitable endpoints.
panels.title: Scheduling Attempt Success Rate

panels.description: End-to-end EPP scheduling latency (P99). Measures time from request arrival at the EPP to endpoint selection. High values indicate scheduler bottleneck.
panels.title: EPP Scheduling Latency P99

panels.description: Plugin processing latency (P99) broken down by plugin type. Shows which scheduler plugins contribute most to scheduling overhead.
panels.title: Plugin Processing Latency P99

panels.description: Flow control queue duration (P99). Shows how long requests wait in the flow control queue before being admitted. Requires FeatureGate enabled. High values indicate backpressure from downstream endpoints.
panels.title: Flow Control Queue Duration P99

panels.description: Rate of failed NIXL KV cache transfers and notifications by model. Any sustained failures indicate RDMA/network issues in the disaggregated serving path.
panels.title: NIXL Failed Transfers & Notifications

panels.description: Rate of requests whose KV cache expired before the decode instance consumed it. Indicates timing mismatch between prefill and decode in disaggregated serving.
panels.title: NIXL KV Expired Requests

panels.description: Number of engines in each sleep state. Sleeping engines (weights_offloaded or discard_all) save GPU memory but require cold-start on wakeup, causing latency spikes.
panels.title: Engine Sleep State Summary

panels.description: Rate of cached prompt tokens vs recomputed tokens. High recomputed rate indicates cache invalidation or prefix mismatches that force redundant computation.
panels.title: Prompt Tokens: Cached vs Recomputed

title: AI / Model Serving / LLMs / Failure & Diagnostics
